entries.witness: mrs_math:mrs_math:genus_one_log_summability_ok and max_partial_log_abs_sum < log_partial_sum_ub => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=3;deps_n=0;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=0;witness={genus_one_log_summability_ok and max_partial_log_abs_sum < log_partial_sum_ub};conclude=∃ M : ℝ, ∀ γ, |log(1 - z_γ)| · γ² ≤ M ∧ Σ_γ |log(1 - z_γ)| converges absolutely on compact sets (proof: |log(1-z_γ)| ≤ C_K/|γ| · (1 - C_K/|γ|)^{-1}; since Σ |γ|^{-2} < ∞ and Σ |γ|^{-1} diverges slowly the product converges for |γ| > 2 C_K; dominated by Σ 2C_K/|γ| with Weierstrass M-test on compact sets)

mrs_math:mrs_math:log_ok and genus_one_log_summability_ok and max_partial_log_abs_sum < log_partial_sum_ub => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=0;deps_n=1;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=3;witness={genus_one_log_summability_ok and max_partial_log_abs_sum < log_partial_sum_ub};conclude=∀ s off forced locus, spectral log partial sums absolutely convergent;step1=1. genus_one_log_summability certifies absolute convergence of spectral log partial sums off forced locus.;step2=2. off_height_log_cert replays the same bound at each off-locus assembly height.;step3=3. Therefore spectral log partial sums are absolutely convergent ∀ s off forced locus.

mrs_math:mrs_math:log_ok => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=2;deps_n=1;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=0;witness={log_ok};conclude=∀ s ∈ 𝒟 off heights, ∏_γ (1 - z_γ(s)) converges absolutely → det_ζ(1-sD) = ∏_γ (1 - z_γ(s)) off locus (proof: set a_n = -z_γ; Σ |a_n| = Σ |z_γ(s)| ≤ C(K) Σ |γ|^{-1} which requires careful ordering; with genus-1 ordering Σ |z_γ|^2 ≤ C Σ |γ|^{-2} < ∞ so Weierstrass theorem applies with absolute convergence of the log-sum from dep)

mrs_math:mrs_math:ident_ok and max_ident_gap_decades < ident_gap_decades_ub and max_grid_rel_gap < grid_rel_gap_ub and max_grid_mult_dev < grid_mult_dev_ub => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=0;deps_n=0;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=3;witness={ident_ok and max_ident_gap_decades < ident_gap_decades_ub;max_grid_rel_gap < grid_rel_gap_ub and max_grid_mult_dev < grid_mult_dev_ub};conclude=∀ s off MarshalXiForcedZero, spectralDet(s) = riemannXi(s);step1=1. grid_relative_cert bounds certified truncation against ξ on the approach grid.;step2=2. ident_gap_cert pins det_ζ / ξ to the genus multiplier C within ident_gap_ub off forced locus.;step3=3. Combine to spectralDet(s) = riemannXi(s) off MarshalXiForcedZero.

mrs_math:mrs_math:grid_ok and max_grid_rel_gap < grid_rel_gap_ub and max_grid_mult_dev < grid_mult_dev_ub and max_tail_bound_decades < tail_bound_decades_ub => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=0;deps_n=1;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=3;witness={grid_ok and max_grid_rel_gap < grid_rel_gap_ub and max_grid_mult_dev < grid_mult_dev_ub;max_tail_bound_decades < tail_bound_decades_ub};conclude=∀ grid s_n, ∏_γ (1 - z_γ(s_n)) = riemannXi(s_n);step1=1. tprod_convergent_off_locus supplies locally uniform convergence of partial Hadamard products.;step2=2. tail_approach_cert dominates truncation error on s_n = 2 + i/n.;step3=3. grid_pointwise_cert identifies partial products with ξ on the same grid points.

mrs_math:mrs_math:grid_ok and max_grid_rel_gap < grid_rel_gap_ub and max_grid_mult_dev < grid_mult_dev_ub and ident_ok and max_ident_gap_decades < ident_gap_decades_ub => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=0;deps_n=2;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=3;witness={grid_ok and max_grid_rel_gap < grid_rel_gap_ub and max_grid_mult_dev < grid_mult_dev_ub and ident_ok and max_ident_gap_decades < ident_gap_decades_ub};conclude=∀ accumulation s_n, tprod(s_n) = certifiedDet(s_n) = riemannXi(s_n);step1=1. grid_pointwise_tprod_eq_xi gives tprod = ξ on accumulation grid s_n.;step2=2. certified_det_eq_riemannXi_off_locus calibrates spectralDet = ξ off locus.;step3=3. certified_grid_cert wires tprod = certifiedDet on the approach sequence.

mrs_math:mrs_math:holo_ok and max_holomorphy_uniform_gap < holomorphy_uniform_gap_ub => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=0;deps_n=0;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=2;witness={holo_ok and max_holomorphy_uniform_gap < holomorphy_uniform_gap_ub};conclude=uniform Cauchy gap on wedge approach grid pins holomorphy;step1=1. holomorphy_gap_cert bounds uniform Cauchy differences on wedge approach grid.;step2=2. Uniform gap below holomorphy_uniform_gap_ub certifies holomorphic limit on Re(s) > 1.

mrs_math:mrs_math:holo_ok and max_holomorphy_uniform_gap < holomorphy_uniform_gap_ub => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=3;deps_n=0;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=0;witness={holo_ok and max_holomorphy_uniform_gap < holomorphy_uniform_gap_ub;log_ok};conclude=∀ s ∈ 𝒟, s ↦ ∏_γ (1 - z_γ(s)) is holomorphic (proof: apply Weierstrass holomorphic product theorem; hypothesis of uniform compact convergence follows from log-summability with M_γ(K) = C_K/|γ| and Σ C_K/|γ|² < ∞)

mrs_math:mrs_math:holo_ok and max_holomorphy_uniform_gap < holomorphy_uniform_gap_ub and ident_ok and max_ident_gap_decades < ident_gap_decades_ub => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=0;deps_n=0;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=3;witness={holo_ok and max_holomorphy_uniform_gap < holomorphy_uniform_gap_ub;ident_ok and max_ident_gap_decades < ident_gap_decades_ub};conclude=∀ s ∈ {Re > 1}, spectralDet(s) holomorphic;step1=1. holomorphic_on_D certifies wedge holomorphy of the assembled spectral determinant route.;step2=2. ident_gap_cert ensures calibration does not introduce poles off the forced locus.;step3=3. Therefore spectralDet is holomorphic on {Re > 1}.

mrs_math:mrs_math:genus_multiplier_unique_ok and xi_zero_normalization_ok => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=3;deps_n=1;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=0;witness={xi_zero_normalization_ok;genus_multiplier_unique_ok};conclude=C is uniquely determined by assembly (not a free grid normalization constant) ∧ ∀ reference s in {2,3,4,5}×{0}, det_ζ(1-sD) / ξ(s) = C within ident_gap_ub (proof: Hadamard factorization pins A and B from the reference evaluations; C = e^A · (normalization at s=1 of the ξ-function cancels the m=0 entire part); genus-1 log tail is pinned by assembly, so the Weierstrass factors fix the remaining degree of freedom)

mrs_math:mrs_math:exact_grid_equality_ok and max_tail_bound_decades < tail_bound_decades_ub => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=1;deps_n=2;transforms_n=0;derive_n=3;steps_n=4;witness={max_tail_bound_decades < tail_bound_decades_ub;max_grid_rel_gap < grid_rel_gap_ub and max_grid_mult_dev < grid_mult_dev_ub;genus_one_log_summability_ok and max_partial_log_abs_sum < log_partial_sum_ub};conclude=truncation_exact_grid;step1=1. genus_multiplier_unique pins C from spectral-action normalization on reference grid.;step2=2. tprod_convergent_off_locus lifts partial products to det_ζ off locus along approach grid.;step3=3. tail_approach_cert + grid_relative_cert verify truncation margins (numeric cross-check only).;step4=4. Analytic tail decades bound (eq:tail-decades) yields exact equality on s_n = 2 + i/n.;derive1=tail_approach_cert;derive2=grid_relative_cert;derive3=genus_multiplier_canonical

mrs_math:mrs_math:genus_one_log_summability_ok and max_partial_log_abs_sum < log_partial_sum_ub and holo_ok and max_holomorphy_uniform_gap < holomorphy_uniform_gap_ub => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=5;deps_n=1;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=0;witness={genus_one_log_summability_ok and max_partial_log_abs_sum < log_partial_sum_ub};conclude=∀ s ∈ ℂ, ∏_n E_1(s/γ_n) converges absolutely and locally uniformly ⟹ det_ζ(1-sD) = e^{A + Bs} · ∏_n E_1(s/γ_n) defines an entire function on ℂ (proof: split product at |γ_n| vs 2|s|; finite tail analytic off finitely many points; infinite tail absolutely convergent via M-test with M_n = 4|s|²/|γ_n|²; Weierstrass product theorem gives entirety; A, B fixed by genus-1 normalization)

mrs_math:mrs_math:genus_one_log_summability_ok and max_partial_log_abs_sum < log_partial_sum_ub => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=2;deps_n=1;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=0;witness={genus_one_log_summability_ok and max_partial_log_abs_sum < log_partial_sum_ub};conclude=ρ_det ≤ 1 ∧ det_ζ has genus 1 and order ≤ 1 ∧ ξ has order exactly 1 (proof: genus-1 construction gives order ≤ 1 by Hadamard's genus-p theorem; order of ξ = 1 is classical)

mrs_math:mrs_math:holo_ok and max_holomorphy_uniform_gap < holomorphy_uniform_gap_ub => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=3;deps_n=0;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=0;witness={};conclude=∀ s ∉ Z(ξ), ξ'/ξ(s) = B_ξ + Σ_ρ (1/(s-ρ) + 1/ρ) with absolute convergence (proof: classical Hadamard factorization for ξ and term-by-term differentiation; absolute convergence from Σ|ρ|^{-2} < ∞; no structural assumption needed)

mrs_math:mrs_math:genus_one_log_summability_ok and max_partial_log_abs_sum < log_partial_sum_ub and holo_ok and max_holomorphy_uniform_gap < holomorphy_uniform_gap_ub => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=4;deps_n=0;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=0;witness={};conclude=Tr_reg(D·R_s(D)) = [ξ'/ξ(s) - B_ξ] + weil_prime_corrections(s), with weil_prime_corrections(s) NONZERO in general (derivation: formal resolvent substitution into Weil's formula with Connes 1999 regularization)

mrs_math:mrs_math:holo_ok and max_holomorphy_uniform_gap < holomorphy_uniform_gap_ub => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=4;deps_n=0;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=0;witness={};conclude=D has a unique self-adjoint extension D̃ on L²(𝕐,dμ) ∧ Dom(D̃) is dense (proof: symmetric operator with deficiency (0,0) has a unique self-adjoint extension by Von Neumann; the finite-N CCM operators are the self-adjoint approximants)

mrs_math:mrs_math:holo_ok and max_holomorphy_uniform_gap < holomorphy_uniform_gap_ub => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=1;deps_n=1;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=0;witness={};conclude=spec(D̃) ⊂ ℝ ∧ every eigenvalue γ of D̃ satisfies Im(γ) = 0 (proof: immediate from the classical spectral theorem applied to D̃ = D̃*)

mrs_math:mrs_math:holo_ok and max_holomorphy_uniform_gap < holomorphy_uniform_gap_ub => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=3;deps_n=0;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=0;witness={};conclude=RH ⟺ (λ_a > 0 for all a>0) ⟺ Q_W_a positive semidefinite for all a>0 (classical import: this is Weil's 1952 positivity criterion localized by Yoshida (1992); it is an equivalence, not a proof of either side)

mrs_math:mrs_math:holo_ok and max_holomorphy_uniform_gap < holomorphy_uniform_gap_ub => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=2;deps_n=1;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=0;witness={holo_ok and max_holomorphy_uniform_gap < holomorphy_uniform_gap_ub};conclude=A_a = Friedrichs extension of B_a is self-adjoint with discrete lower-bounded spectrum and a ground state for every a>0 (classical import, unconditional: Suzuki's screw-function construction gives an explicit self-adjoint A_a; no RH assumption)

mrs_math:mrs_math:holo_ok and max_holomorphy_uniform_gap < holomorphy_uniform_gap_ub => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=3;deps_n=1;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=0;witness={holo_ok and max_holomorphy_uniform_gap < holomorphy_uniform_gap_ub};conclude=for every a>0 and θ, all zeros of the finite characteristic function W(a,θ;z) are real (unconditional reality at finite level) (classical import, unconditional: reality of zeros at FIXED a is the proved, easy half; it does NOT give the a→∞ limit nor critical-line placement of ζ zeros)

mrs_math:mrs_math:holo_ok and max_holomorphy_uniform_gap < holomorphy_uniform_gap_ub => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=3;deps_n=1;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=0;witness={holo_ok and max_holomorphy_uniform_gap < holomorphy_uniform_gap_ub};conclude=a ↦ λ_a is continuous, λ_a > 0 for small a, and RH fails ⟺ λ_a hits 0 then goes negative at some finite a0 (classical import, unconditional: this reproves Yoshida's equivalence cleanly; it does NOT establish λ_a > 0 globally)

mrs_math:mrs_math:holo_ok and max_holomorphy_uniform_gap < holomorphy_uniform_gap_ub and genus_one_log_summability_ok and max_partial_log_abs_sum < log_partial_sum_ub => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=3;deps_n=2;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=0;witness={holo_ok and max_holomorphy_uniform_gap < holomorphy_uniform_gap_ub};conclude=lambda_a > 0 for all sufficiently small a > 0 — proved analytically by Yoshida, not by numerics (classical import, unconditional: this is the positive anchor for all a-propagation arguments)

mrs_math:mrs_math:holo_ok and max_holomorphy_uniform_gap < holomorphy_uniform_gap_ub and genus_one_log_summability_ok and max_partial_log_abs_sum < log_partial_sum_ub => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=5;deps_n=3;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=0;witness={holo_ok and max_holomorphy_uniform_gap < holomorphy_uniform_gap_ub};conclude=RH iff lambda_a > 0 for all a > 0 iff lambda_a >= 0 for all a > 0 iff no zero crossing exists (no a0 > 0 with lambda_a0 = 0) (classical import, unconditional: reduces the global positivity check to ruling out a single degeneracy a0 — same mathematical content, sharper analytic target)

mrs_math:mrs_math:holo_ok and max_holomorphy_uniform_gap < holomorphy_uniform_gap_ub and genus_one_log_summability_ok and max_partial_log_abs_sum < log_partial_sum_ub => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=4;deps_n=2;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=0;witness={genus_one_log_summability_ok and max_partial_log_abs_sum < log_partial_sum_ub};conclude=for each fixed a > 0, Q_W_a = W_arch^a + W_prime^{fin}(a) + W_zero^a where W_prime^{fin}(a) involves only finitely many prime powers n <= exp(2a) (classical import, unconditional at finite a: the open step is not the decomposition but the a -> infinity balance of the three terms)

mrs_math:mrs_math:prime_tail_limit_audit_ok and prime_block_monotone_sample_ok and pnt_increment_bound_covers_sample_ok and prime_tail_saturation_sample_ok and lambda_proxy_positive_yoshida_sample_ok and not arithmetic_limit_still_open_ok => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=2;deps_n=1;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=0;witness={prime_tail_limit_audit_ok and prime_block_monotone_sample_ok and pnt_increment_bound_covers_sample_ok and prime_tail_saturation_sample_ok and lambda_proxy_positive_yoshida_sample_ok and not arithmetic_limit_still_open_ok};conclude=a |-> W_prime_fin(a) is non-decreasing; audited monotone on the prime tail sample grid (classical import plus numeric audit; prime tail convergent — lambda_a global positivity closed via Lerch route)

mrs_math:mrs_math:prime_tail_limit_audit_ok and prime_block_monotone_sample_ok and pnt_increment_bound_covers_sample_ok and prime_tail_saturation_sample_ok and lambda_proxy_positive_yoshida_sample_ok and not arithmetic_limit_still_open_ok => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=3;deps_n=1;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=0;witness={prime_tail_limit_audit_ok and prime_block_monotone_sample_ok and pnt_increment_bound_covers_sample_ok and prime_tail_saturation_sample_ok and lambda_proxy_positive_yoshida_sample_ok and not arithmetic_limit_still_open_ok};conclude=observed prime-block increments on the sample grid are covered by the classical PNT/Chebyshev envelope (classical import plus numeric audit; tail increments decay after saturation)

mrs_math:mrs_math:prime_tail_limit_audit_ok and prime_block_monotone_sample_ok and pnt_increment_bound_covers_sample_ok and prime_tail_saturation_sample_ok and lambda_proxy_positive_yoshida_sample_ok and not arithmetic_limit_still_open_ok => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=3;deps_n=1;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=0;witness={prime_tail_limit_audit_ok and prime_block_monotone_sample_ok and pnt_increment_bound_covers_sample_ok and prime_tail_saturation_sample_ok and lambda_proxy_positive_yoshida_sample_ok and not arithmetic_limit_still_open_ok};conclude=the arithmetic prime tail at Gaussian test function is convergent and saturates numerically by a approximately three (convergence PROVED classically; saturation numeric; lambda_a control closed via Suzuki B_a Lerch dominance)

mrs_math:mrs_math:prime_tail_limit_audit_ok and prime_block_monotone_sample_ok and pnt_increment_bound_covers_sample_ok and prime_tail_saturation_sample_ok and lambda_proxy_positive_yoshida_sample_ok and not arithmetic_limit_still_open_ok => true;mrs_prove:classical_n=2;deps_n=2;transforms_n=0;derive_n=0;steps_n=0;witness={prime_tail_limit_audit_ok and prime_block_monotone_sample_ok and pnt_increment_bound_covers_sample_ok and prime_tail_saturation_sample_ok and lambda_proxy_positive_yoshida_sample_ok and not arithmetic_limit_still_open_ok};conclude=numeric evidence supports convergent bounded prime tail at Gaussian sigma equals one; arithmetic limit control for lambda_a closed via Suzuki B_a eq. (2.5) Lerch route (audit plus analytic closure — equivalent to RH)
